macro_segments.title: The Requirement of Standing Beside One's Field and the Distinction Between Chatzer Mishtameret and Chatzer Lo Mishtameret

micro_segments.title: Introduction to the Mishna and Background on Previous Discussion
Rav Yehuda's Teaching on Standing Alongside the Field and the Challenge from Rabbi Yose bar Chanina
The Gemara's Distinction Between Chatzer Mishtameret and Chatzer Lo Mishtameret
Rashi's Explanation and Alternative Understanding Through Yad versus Shlichut

macro_segments.title: The Case of the Rabbis on the Boat: Transferring Maaser Through Kinyan Chatzer and the Role of Da'at Acheret

micro_segments.title: The Scenario of Rabban Gamliel and the Rabbis Transferring Maaser Grain Through Renting Space
The Gemara's Challenge: Why the Field Works Without Someone Standing Nearby
Two Possible Answers: Kinyan Agav versus Da'at Acheret Makneh
Rav Papa's Resolution: When Another's Intent Transfers an Object, Looser Possession Suffices

macro_segments.title: Da'at Acheret Makneh: The Principle That Another's Intent to Transfer Lowers Requirements for Kinyan

micro_segments.title: Source for Da'at Acheret Makneh from the Mishna on Chasing Deer
Rabbi Yirmeyah's Question and Clarification on When a Deer Can Be Seized
Rabbi Yirmeyah's Question About Matanah and Rabbi Abba bar Kahana's Understanding
Drawing the Distinction: Matanah Works Loosely with Da'at Acheret, Unlike Metzia

macro_segments.title: The Case of Get and the Limitation of Da'at Acheret: When the Transfer is to Someone's Detriment

micro_segments.title: Rav Shimi's Challenge: The Case of Get Where Even Da'at Acheret May Not Suffice
Ulla's Ruling: Get Requires Standing Beside the House Even with Da'at Acheret
The Gemara's Distinction: Get is Different Because It Can Be Forced on the Woman
Rav Sheshet's Counter-Kal Vachomer: Gift Should Require Even Stronger Standing than Forced Get

macro_segments.title: Rav Ashi's Clarification: Chatzer Functions as Yad or Shaliach, and When Each Applies

micro_segments.title: Chatzer Can Function as Yad (When Standing Beside) or as Shaliach (When Not Standing)
The Principle of Ein Chavin L'Adam Ella Befanav: When Shlichus Cannot Act for Detriment
Why Get Cannot Use Shlichus: Get to Detriment Cannot Use Chatzer as Agent
Three Categories: Get (Always Needs Yad), Matanah (Works as Shaliach Loosely), Metzia (Needs Either Mishtameret or Standing)

macro_segments.title: Application of Da'at Acheret and Standing Requirements: Flying Wallet and Rolling Objects

micro_segments.title: Rava's Question: Does a Wallet Flying Through Airspace Count as in the Yard?
Response: Distinction Between Flying (Not Present), Rolling (Present Like Stationary), and Landing
Application: Deer and Objects Flying Through Yard Work Differently for Metzia vs Matanah

macro_segments.title: Metzia of Household Members: General Principles and the Case of Minor Son

micro_segments.title: The Mishna: Wife, Minor Children, Slaves, and Their Findings Go to the Father/Owner
Rationale for Wife: Reciprocal Rights and Responsibilities in Marriage
Rationale for Minor Daughter: Preventing Father's Anger and Abuse of Authority
Rationale for Slaves: They Are Extensions of Owner Like Chatzer
Question Posed: Why Minor Son Specifically Gets Findings

macro_segments.title: Shmuel's Explanation of Minor Son: The Child Immediately Brings Finding to Father Without Taking Possession

micro_segments.title: Shmuel's Reasoning: Minor Runs to Father and Never Takes Possession Himself
Implicit Question: Does This Imply a Minor Never Has Zachiya on His Own?

macro_segments.title: The Braisa on Sharecropper's Child Collecting Leket and the Halakha According to Rabbi Yose

micro_segments.title: Braisa: Worker Hired to Gather Can Have Child Collect Leket; Sharecropper Cannot
Reason: Sharecropper (Even As Ani) Cannot Collect Leket; Worker Can Have Child Collect
Rabbi Yose's Dissent: Child Collects for Themselves, Not Father, So Works Even for Sharecropper
Shmuel Rules Like Rabbi Yose, Leading to a Problem with Shmuel's Explanation

macro_segments.title: Resolving Shmuel's Position: Does the Minor Take Possession First, Then Transfer to Father?

micro_segments.title: If Minor Has Zachiya, He First Takes Possession, Then It Transfers to Father via Rabbinic Law
This Would Solve the Sharecropper Problem: Child Takes Possession (As Ani) Then Transfers
But Shmuel Apparently Rejects This: Minor Never Takes Possession, Goes Straight to Father
Gemara's Clarification: Shmuel Explains the Mishna, Not His Own View; He Personally Holds Child First

macro_segments.title: The Braisa on Deaf, Imbecile, and Minor Finding Objects and the Principle of Darchei Shalom

micro_segments.title: Mishna: Metzia of Deaf, Imbecile, and Minor—No Gezel But Darchei Shalom Permits Them to Keep
Rabbi Yose: It Is Actual Gezel, Not Just Darchei Shalom
Rav Chisda's Clarification: Rabbi Yose Means Rabbinically It Is Gezel Because of Darchei Shalom
The Nafka Mina: Whether Victim Can Sue to Recover the Object in Court

macro_segments.title: Abaye's Explanation of Rabbi Yose on Leket: The Aniyim Despair and Give Up Collecting

micro_segments.title: Abaye: The Leket Is Like When Second-Round Gleaners Have Already Passed, Aniyim No Longer Bother
Application: Aniyim Figure the Sharecropper's Child Will Gather Everything, So They Despair
Therefore Leket Is No Longer Restricted to Aniyim; Others Can Take It, Including Owner

macro_segments.title: Rav Adda's Challenge to Abaye: Can One Deliberately Drive Away the Aniyim Using Force?

micro_segments.title: Rav Adda's Kal Vachomer: Surely One Cannot Put Lions in Field to Scare Away Aniyim
This Would Undermine Abaye's Explanation If Despair Alone Made Leket Available

macro_segments.title: Rava's Resolution: The Rabbis Made an Exception for Minor to Actually Acquire (Not Just Darchei Shalom)

micro_segments.title: Rava: The Mishna Rule Is That Minor Can Actually Take Possession (Zachiya), Not Just Darchei Shalom
This Is a Rabbinic Enactment (Asu) to Allow Minor Full Acquisition in Leket
Reason: Aniyim Benefit from This Policy When They Are Hired to Gather Grain

macro_segments.title: Rabbi Hiyya bar Abba's Alternative: Possession Goes to Whoever Is Supported by the Father, Not Age

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Hiyya bar Abba in Name of Rabbi Yochanan: Not About Adult vs Minor, But About Dependence
If Supported by Father, Father Gets Metzia; If Independent, Person Keeps It, Regardless of Age
Reason: Maintain Good Relations; Don't Want Supported Child to Anger Father
This Mechanism (Own First, Then Transfer) Is Compatible with the Leket Case

macro_segments.title: Metzia of Hebrew Slaves: Why Do They Keep Findings Contrary to Expected Ownership?

micro_segments.title: Mishna: Hebrew Slave's Findings Go to Slave, Not Owner
Gemara's Question: Why Not Worse Than Hired Hand, Whom We Also Said Keeps It?
Braisa: Hired Hand Keeps Metzia if Off-Clock; Gets Owner's Metzia if Told to Work Whole Day

macro_segments.title: The Case of Specialized Slave and Different Mechanisms for Slave Metzia Ownership

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Hiyya bar Abba: We Discuss Slave with Specialized Skill (Pearl Drilling) Whom Master Won't Reassign
When Slave Stops Drilling Pearls to Collect Metzia, He Is Off-Clock and Must Reimburse Master
Rava's Alternative: Slave Collecting Metzia While Doing Other Work (Like Gathering Garbage)
The Act of Collecting Is Not for Master, So Slave Keeps It Even Though Employed by Master

macro_segments.title: Rav Pappa's Clarification: The Braisa on Hired Hand Collecting Metzia Applies Only to Specialized Hiring

micro_segments.title: Rav Pappa Reinterprets Braisa: The 'Work Whole Day' Case Means Hired Specifically to Collect Metzi'os
Example: Hiring Worker to Collect Fish After Flood—Then Metzi'os Go to Employer
Generic Hiring Without Specific Task Designation: Worker Keeps Metzia

macro_segments.title: Metzia of Hebrew Slave Girl: Who Gets It When Father Is Alive Versus Dead?

micro_segments.title: Question: If Slave Girl Has Two Pubic Hairs She Is Adult and Goes Free; If Not, Father Might Own
Gemara: If Minor and Father Alive, Metzia Should Go to Father (Not Master); Why Does Mishna Say She Keeps It?
Reish Lakish's Teaching: Slave Girl Goes Free Upon Father's Death (Disputed in Gemara)
Gemara's Resolution: Mishna Discusses Father Alive; She Still Keeps Metzia First, Then It Goes to Father

macro_segments.title: Metzia of Divorced Wife: When Relationship Is Severed, Wife Keeps Findings Despite Divorce Uncertainty

micro_segments.title: Mishna: Divorced Wife's Findings Go to Her, Not Husband, Even If Ketubah Not Yet Paid
Gemara: If Divorce Status Is Uncertain, Husband Still Obligated to Support Her
Reason Normally Wife's Metzia Goes to Husband: Maintain Good Relations, Prevent Enmity
In Divorce: Tremendous Enmity Exists; No Reciprocal Obligation on Wife, So She Keeps Metzia

topical_tags.term: Rav Yehuda in name of Shmuel
Rabban Gamliel and Rabbis on Boat
